patch.value: Minor UX/correctness: test --add wording, dep-kind vocab, same-command did-you-mean, plan materialize, close inline resolution, scaffold defineExtension

From dogfood (pm-gqx7) + SDK review (pm-a0w4). (3) 'pm test --add' block message says 'set both PM_PATH and PM_GLOBAL_PATH' but exporting them does NOT satisfy the guard — must be INLINE in the command string (test.ts:659). Fix wording. (4) Dependency kind vocab differs: pm plan uses depends_on but 'pm update --dep kind=depends_on' rejects it (wants blocked_by/blocks/...) — accept depends_on alias or unify. (6) No same-command did-you-mean for flag typos ('pm create --titel'). (10) 'pm plan materialize --steps all' materializes already-completed steps as fresh open Tasks. (11) 'pm close <id> <reason> --resolution' not accepted inline (must update first). (B-1) Generated package scaffold (scaffold.ts:38-56) uses bare 'export function activate(api)' and does not import defineExtension, so its peerDependencies type help is unused — scaffold should use defineExtension like the docs example.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Implemented all six on feat/agent-ux-compact-output-and-mutation-guards: (#3) test --add wording now says "INLINE in the command string" with example (test.ts). (#4) depends_on accepted as input alias mapping to canonical blocked_by on both pm update --dep and --dep-remove via normalizeDependencyKindInput (update.ts). (#6) src/core/shared/levenshtein.ts upgraded to OSA Damerau-Levenshtein — single adjacent transpositions (titel/title, lst/list, teh/the) now cost 1, so bootstrap-args silently auto-corrects them AND commander-usage suggestNearestLongFlags surfaces them in errors. (#10) plan materialize now skips completed and already-materialized steps via resolveMaterializeTargets returning {targets, skipped}; second --steps all is idempotent with helpful error; explicit refs report materialize_skipped + warnings; new PlanCommandResult.materialize_skipped field. (#11) close.ts accepts inline --resolution/--expected-result/--actual-result (kebab + snake aliases) applied before the validation pass so a single pm close call now satisfies --validate-close strict. (B-1) extension scaffold emits import { defineExtension } from "@unbrained/pm-cli/sdk" + export default defineExtension({ activate(api) {...} }) matching docs/EXTENSIONS.md and docs/SDK.md exactly. New tests: levenshtein.spec.ts (covers OSA Damerau + post-loop guard branch with abcd/abcyy@1), plan-command idempotency tests, close-command inline-fields test, depends_on alias test, scaffold defineExtension assertions, help-runtime --titel auto-correct + pm create Epic guard tests. 1866 tests + 100% coverage.

patch.value: Six sub-fixes across test.ts, update.ts, levenshtein.ts, plan.ts, close.ts + register-mutation.ts, and extension/scaffold.ts. New levenshtein.spec.ts asserts adjacent-transposition cost=1 and exercises the post-loop overflow branch.

Each sub-issue produces the documented agent UX: (3) helpful wording, (4) depends_on accepted, (6) typo auto-correct or suggestion, (10) idempotent materialize, (11) one-shot close validation, (B-1) defineExtension shape.

Smoke-tested all six in /tmp/pm-e2e: test --add error contains 'INLINE in the command string'; --dep kind=depends_on stores as canonical blocked_by; pm create --titel auto-corrects to --title pre-commander; second plan materialize --steps all is idempotent with code=already_materialized; pm close --resolution/--expected-result/--actual-result accepted in one call and satisfies --validate-close strict; new scaffold index.js opens with the defineExtension import and wrapper.

Shipped on feat/agent-ux-compact-output-and-mutation-guards (PR pending): test --add wording, depends_on alias, OSA Damerau levenshtein + flag/command typo hints, plan materialize idempotency, close inline closure fields, scaffold defineExtension.
